Introduction
Laravel has become one of the worldтАЩs most popular PHP frameworks. Every year, a new major version introduces improvements in performance, security, developer experience, and modern PHP support.
Many developers ask the same questions:

тАв Should I still use Laravel 10?

тАв Is Laravel 11 better?

тАв Is Laravel 12 stable?

тАв Should I start a new project with Laravel 13?

тАв What PHP version do I need?

тАв Will my existing packages work?
If youтАЩre confused, this guide will help you choose the right version.

Laravel Release Timeline
VersionRelease YearBest ForLaravel 102023Enterprise & LTS ProjectsLaravel 112024Modern ApplicationsLaravel 122025Production ProjectsLaravel 132026Latest Applications
Laravel releases a new major version every year while continuing to improve developer productivity and adopting the latest PHP features.

PHP Requirements
One of the biggest changes between Laravel versions is the minimum PHP requirement.
LaravelMinimum PHPRecommended PHPLaravel 10PHP 8.1PHP 8.2Laravel 11PHP 8.2PHP 8.3Laravel 12PHP 8.2PHP 8.3Laravel 13PHP 8.3PHP 8.4
Why does this matter?
Every newer PHP version brings:

тАв Better performance

тАв Lower memory usage

тАв Improved type safety

тАв Modern language features

тАв Stronger security

тАв Faster execution
In many applications, upgrading PHP alone provides noticeable performance improvements.

Composer Requirements
Laravel also evolves with Composer.
LaravelComposerLaravel 102.5+Laravel 112.6+Laravel 122.7+Laravel 13Latest Composer 2.x
Keeping Composer updated ensures better dependency resolution and compatibility with the latest packages.

Project Structure Evolution
Laravel 10
Laravel 10 follows the traditional structure developers have used for years.
app/
bootstrap/
config/
database/
public/
resources/
routes/
storage/Everything is separated into dedicated files, making it familiar for experienced developers.

Laravel 11
Laravel 11 introduced the biggest structural change in recent years.
The framework became significantly simpler.
Several configuration files are no longer generated by default.
Middleware registration moved to:
bootstrap/app.phpinstead of:
app/Http/Kernel.phpThis reduces boilerplate and makes new projects easier to understand.

Laravel 12
Laravel 12 keeps Laravel 11тАЩs simplified structure while improving developer experience and polishing the framework.
No major architectural changes were introduced.

Laravel 13
Laravel 13 continues refining the streamlined structure, updates dependencies, and improves support for the latest PHP versions.

Performance Comparison
Performance is a common reason developers consider upgrading.
VersionPerformanceLaravel 10тнРтнРтнРтнРLaravel 11тнРтнРтнРтнРтнРLaravel 12тнРтнРтнРтнРтнРLaravel 13тнРтнРтнРтнРтнР
However, remember:
A poorly optimized Laravel 13 application can still be slower than a well-optimized Laravel 10 application.
Real performance depends on:

тАв Database optimization

тАв Redis caching

тАв Queue workers

тАв Efficient queries

тАв Proper indexing

тАв Server configuration

Authentication
Laravel offers starter kits to simplify authentication.
Laravel 10

тАв Laravel Breeze

тАв Laravel Jetstream
Laravel 11
Updated starter kits with a cleaner setup.
Laravel 12
Further improvements to authentication workflows and package compatibility.
Laravel 13
Latest starter kits with support for modern Laravel conventions.

Security
Security remains one of LaravelтАЩs strongest features across all versions.
Each version includes:

тАв CSRF Protection

тАв XSS Protection

тАв SQL Injection Protection

тАв Password Hashing

тАв Email Verification

тАв Authorization Policies

тАв Rate Limiting

тАв Encryption
Newer versions receive updates aligned with newer PHP versions and dependencies.

Developer Experience
Laravel has always focused on developer happiness.
Laravel 10

тАв Stable

тАв Predictable

тАв Mature ecosystem
Laravel 11

тАв Cleaner setup

тАв Less boilerplate

тАв Faster project creation
Laravel 12

тАв More polished

тАв Better testing support

тАв Improved ecosystem integration
Laravel 13

тАв Latest tooling

тАв Modern PHP features

тАв Ongoing ecosystem updates

Ecosystem Compatibility
Before upgrading, always check whether your packages support the target version.
Popular packages include:

тАв Livewire

тАв Inertia.js

тАв Sanctum

тАв Passport

тАв Horizon

тАв Telescope

тАв Spatie packages
Waiting for package support can save significant migration effort.

Upgrade Difficulty
UpgradeDifficulty10 тЖТ 11Medium11 тЖТ 12Easy12 тЖТ 13Easy
The transition from Laravel 10 to Laravel 11 typically requires the most attention because of the structural changes.

Which Version Should You Choose?
Beginners
Recommended: Laravel 12 or Laravel 13
They offer a modern project structure and excellent documentation.

Freelancers
Recommended: Laravel 12 or Laravel 13
Ideal for client projects that need current features and long-term maintainability.

Startups
Recommended: Laravel 13
Great for greenfield projects using the latest ecosystem.

Enterprise Projects
Recommended: Laravel 10 (existing projects) or Laravel 12 (new projects)
Upgrade based on business needs rather than version numbers alone.
Government Projects
Stability matters more than adopting the newest release.
Choose the version that aligns with your organizationтАЩs maintenance policies and package compatibility.

Laravel Version Comparison
FeatureLaravel 10Laravel 11Laravel 12Laravel 13StableтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРPerformanceтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРSecurityтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРтнРLearning CurveEasyEasyEasyEasyModern PHP SupportGoodBetterBetterBestEcosystemMatureMatureMatureGrowing

My Recommendation
If I were starting a project today:

тАв Existing Laravel 10 project: Continue using it if it meets your requirements. Upgrade when thereтАЩs a clear benefit.

тАв New production project: Laravel 12 offers an excellent balance of stability and modern features.

тАв Brand-new project with compatible dependencies: Laravel 13 is a strong choice for staying current.
The best version isnтАЩt always the newest one тАФ itтАЩs the one that matches your projectтАЩs goals, team expertise, and dependency support.

Final Thoughts
Laravel continues to evolve without losing its core philosophy: building modern web applications should be elegant, enjoyable, and productive.
Whether youтАЩre maintaining a legacy enterprise application or launching the next SaaS product, understanding the differences between Laravel 10, 11, 12, and 13 will help you make informed technical decisions.
At the end of the day, good architecture, clean code, testing, security, and performance optimization matter far more than simply using the latest version.
